review_method: Checked out 31ad5c1 in an isolated git worktree (separate from the fixer's own worktree agent-ac141fd24d0de95f8), ran targeted + full backend pytest against DATABASE_URL_TEST (aether_test schema), full frontend vitest, tsc --noEmit, and next build. Additionally swapped in the PRE-fix router body (from 31ad5c1^) under the NEW test file to prove the two referential-check tests are non-tautological (they fail against old code, pass against new code), then restored the fixed router.

list_wired_real.detail: apps/web/src/app/dashboard/interviews/page.tsx now calls fetchInterviews() -> GET /interviews on mount via useEffect(load). interviews state starts null (loading), then is set to the real array. The old unconditional 'No interview scheduled.' placeholder block is gone; the empty-state div (data-testid=interviews-empty-state) is now behind `interviews.length === 0` and only renders when the real fetch returned zero rows. vitest test 'GETs /interviews and renders real rows' asserts apiRequest was called with '/interviews', asserts real fixture fields (Senior Product Owner / Acme Corp / prep note text) render inside the card, and asserts the empty-state testid is NOT present when data exists. Ran and PASSED in this session.

create_real_roundtrip.detail: 'Schedule interview' button (data-testid=schedule-interview-btn) opens a real form (data-testid=schedule-interview-form) with an application picker sourced from GET /applications, type/date/duration/location/link/contact/notes fields, and a submit handler that calls createInterview() -> POST /interviews with the built payload, then calls load() to refetch the list. vitest test 'schedules an interview that round-trips into the list, then completes it' drives the form via fireEvent, asserts POST /interviews fired with application_id='app-1' and an ISO scheduled_at, asserts the created interview appears as a real card afterward, and asserts a follow-on status transition (complete) hits POST /interviews/{id}/complete and updates the rendered status. Backend test test_create_interview_with_real_application_succeeds independently confirms the same round-trip server-side (POST 201, then GET /interviews contains the new row). This is a real server round trip, not a client-only optimistic fake — the list only updates because load() re-fetches from the mocked/real apiRequest.

referential_check_404_scoped.detail: apps/api/app/routers/interviews.py new _verify_application_ref(application_id, user_id) runs `SELECT 1 FROM "Application" WHERE "id"=%s AND "userId"=%s` and raises HTTPException(404, 'Referenced application not found') when no row matches; called from create_interview before the INSERT. Confirmed 404 (not 403) is the established codebase convention for ownership-scoped anti-enumeration (apps/api/app/routers/applications.py:124,153 use the identical 404-not-403 pattern for owned-resource lookups), consistent with this router's own pre-existing _get_or_404 helper. Confirmed additive/backward-compatible: no DDL change in the diff (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS InterviewSchedule is untouched), the check only executes inside create_interview (POST), so GET/PATCH/DELETE/complete/cancel and any pre-existing rows are unaffected.

backend_tests_result.targeted_run: cd apps/api && DATABASE_URL=$DATABASE_URL_TEST DATABASE_URL_TEST=<same> AETHER_CREDENTIAL_KEY=*** AETHER_ASYNC_GENERATION=false flock /tmp/aether-pytest.lock python3 -m pytest tests/test_interviews.py -q => 4 passed in 9.83s
backend_tests_result.non_tautology_probe: Swapped in the router body from 31ad5c1^ (pre-fix, no _verify_application_ref) under the identical new test file and re-ran: 2 failed, 2 passed — test_create_interview_with_nonexistent_application_returns_404 and test_create_interview_with_foreign_application_returns_404 FAIL against old code (old code returned 201 for both, matching the fixer's claim), then restored the fixed router and re-ran: 4 passed. This proves the new tests genuinely exercise the fixed behaviour rather than being tautological.
backend_tests_result.regression_run: pytest tests/test_interviews.py tests/test_applications_tracker.py -q => 14 passed in 25.75s (no cross-router regression)
backend_tests_result.note: conftest.py's ProdTruncationGuard required DATABASE_URL_TEST to be explicitly exported (not only DATABASE_URL) or it aborts with 'REFUSING TO RUN' per docs/delivery/INCIDENT-PROD-DB-WIPE-2026-07-18.md; both were set to the aether_test schema DSN, never a prod DSN.

functional_not_placeholder.detail: Screen has: real GET-backed list with role/company (joined from /applications), scheduled time, type, duration, status pill, location/meeting-link/contact/notes when present; a real create form; status transitions (Mark complete / Cancel interview -> POST .../complete and .../cancel) that update the card via the response body; delete with a confirm/keep two-step. Loading state (skeleton, data-testid=interviews-loading) while interviews===null; honest empty state distinct from loading; error state (data-testid via role=alert banner + Retry button) on fetch failure. Scope note (Live Assist real-time coaching and the interview-center compliance/recording-consent banner are deferred) is reasonable and independently corroborated by docs/delivery/PROGRESS.md:457 (P0-S08, a separate compliance-banner line item) and docs/research/competitive_features.md (Live Assist listed as a distinct, larger 'NEW screen' feature) — this is a documented pre-existing scope boundary, not the fixer inventing an excuse to skip work.

vitest_result.targeted: npx vitest run src/app/dashboard/interviews => 1 file, 2 tests passed
vitest_result.full_suite: npx vitest run => 58 files, 407 tests passed, 62.48s (no regressions)
vitest_result.build: npm run build => Next.js 14.2.35 production build succeeded, 29/29 static pages generated, /dashboard/interviews route compiled (4.81 kB)
vitest_result.lint: npx next lint --dir src --file src/app/dashboard/interviews/page.tsx => No ESLint warnings or errors

prohibited_patterns_notes: Scanned the full diff for TODO/FIXME, Math.random, @ts-ignore, eslint-disable, --no-verify, broad `any` casts, console.log/debugger. Found one `// eslint-disable-next-line import/first` in the new vitest test file (apps/web/src/app/dashboard/interviews/__tests__/page.test.tsx) — this is the standard, narrow vitest idiom required because `vi.mock(...)` must be called before importing the component under test for hoisting to work; it disables a purely stylistic import-order rule, not an error-suppression or type-safety escape hatch. Not treated as a violation.
reasons: MV-interview-center-001 (BLOCKER) CLOSED: GET /interviews fires on mount, real rows render, hardcoded placeholder removed and gated correctly behind an honest empty check.

MV-interview-center-003 (BLOCKER) CLOSED: real 'Schedule interview' form POSTs to /interviews scoped to a caller-owned application id selected from a live /applications-backed picker; verified server-side round-trip in both pytest and vitest.

MV-interview-center-004 (MED) CLOSED: _verify_application_ref enforces ownership-scoped referential integrity, 404 on missing/foreign ref, proven non-tautological via before/after test swap, additive/backward-compatible (no DDL change, POST-only check).

MV-interview-center-002 (HIGH) CLOSED: screen is genuinely functional — list, create, status transitions, honest loading/empty/error states; the Live Assist / compliance-banner scope note is independently corroborated as a pre-existing, separately-tracked larger feature, not a fixer excuse.
All required gates green: backend targeted (4/4) and cross-router regression (14/14) pytest, full vitest (407/407), tsc clean, next build clean, lint clean.
No prohibited patterns found reachable by users; the one eslint-disable is a narrow, justified test-tooling idiom (import ordering for mock hoisting), not a suppressed error or type-safety bypass.
concerns: Minor UX rough edge (not a fail reason): in page.tsx's `load()`, a fetchInterviews() failure sets BOTH the red error banner (with Retry) AND `interviews` to `[]`, which also renders the 'No interviews scheduled yet' empty-state card underneath simultaneously. The error is genuinely surfaced (not hidden), but the concurrent empty-state copy could read as contradictory ('no interviews' vs 'failed to load'). Recommend a future polish pass keep interviews as null (or a distinct error-only branch) on fetch failure so the empty-state illustration doesn't co-render with an active error.

_verify_application_ref opens its own DB connection/transaction separate from the subsequent INSERT connection, leaving a small (practically negligible) TOCTOU window where the caller's own application could be deleted between the check and the insert. This matches the router's pre-existing style (every other endpoint in this file already does the same multi-connection pattern for _get_or_404 + mutation), so it is not a regression introduced by this fix, just a residual architectural characteristic worth noting for any future FK-constraint hardening pass.

fetchApplications() failures are silently swallowed ('keep last-known apps') so the create-form's application picker could silently go stale/empty without user-visible feedback if /applications is down while /interviews succeeds; low severity since the picker itself still degrades to an honest 'You need an application first' notice when apps.length===0.
